AlertChannel.description: Where to send SLA breach alerts.
AlertChannel.title: AlertChannel

AlertEvent.description: A record of an SLA breach alert that was sent.
AlertEvent.title: AlertEvent

BestTime.description: The greenest hour-of-day to run a recurring job, for picking a cron schedule.

annual_kg_saved.description: If a daily job of the given --energy-kwh moved from the worst to the best hour: estimated kg CO2 avoided per year (assumes the pattern holds). Null without energy.
annual_kg_saved.title: Annual Kg Saved

basis.description: history (observed hour-of-day means), forecast (next-48h curve as a proxy), or insufficient (no data yet)
basis.title: Basis

cleanest_hour_utc.description: The lowest-mean-intensity UTC hour, or null if no data
cleanest_hour_utc.title: Cleanest Hour Utc

dirtiest_hour_utc.description: The highest-mean-intensity UTC hour, for contrast
dirtiest_hour_utc.title: Dirtiest Hour Utc

shift_savings_pct.description: How much cleaner the best hour is than the worst observed hour (%). The value of scheduling well vs scheduling badly.
shift_savings_pct.title: Shift Savings Pct

suggested_cron.description: A daily crontab line for the cleanest hour (UTC), or null
suggested_cron.title: Suggested Cron

BestTime.title: BestTime

CarbonAnomaly.description: Whether a zone is cleaner or dirtier than its own historical baseline now.
CarbonAnomaly.title: CarbonAnomaly

clean_surplus_hours.description: Hour offsets (0 = now) projected to be clean surplus -- renewables dominant and very low carbon, so extra load likely soaks up power that would otherwise be curtailed. The highest-value windows to shift flexible load into. A heuristic from the projected mix, not measured curtailment.
clean_surplus_hours.title: Clean Surplus Hours

method.description: How the curve was produced: 'entsoe_day_ahead' (real EU day-ahead wind/solar/load forecast) or 'time_of_day_model' (a local time-of-day heuristic, not a measured forecast).
method.title: Method

points.description: Hour-by-hour projection over the horizon; the first point is the current reading.
points.title: Points

points.description: Past readings for this region, oldest first, from the published rolling history archive. Empty until the archive accumulates.
points.title: Points

grid_load_mw.description: Total grid generation/load for the balancing authority in MW (whole grid, all consumers — not datacenter-specific). None when the source does not report it.
grid_load_mw.title: Grid Load Mw

marginal_intensity_gco2_kwh.description: Estimated marginal emission factor (what an extra kWh of demand would emit). A heuristic from the fuel mix, not measured marginal data. None for sources without a real fuel mix.
marginal_intensity_gco2_kwh.title: Marginal Intensity Gco2 Kwh

power_breakdown_mw.description: Live generation breakdown by fuel type in MW (e.g. {"wind": 4200, "natural_gas": 1800, "nuclear": 9500}). Only the fuels actually generating are listed. None for sources without a real fuel mix (heuristic and weather-based estimates).
power_breakdown_mw.title: Power Breakdown Mw

avg_intensity_reduction_gco2_kwh.description: Average per-recommendation carbon-intensity reduction vs the baseline. A rate (gCO2/kWh), not a total: per-kWh intensities aren't additive across workloads, and real grams also depend on each job's energy use.
avg_intensity_reduction_gco2_kwh.title: Avg Intensity Reduction Gco2 Kwh

baseline.description: The counterfactual the reduction is measured against.
baseline.title: Baseline

CarbonSignal.description:
A one-call decision primitive: should a flexible job run here now, or wait?

Designed for the carbon-aware-dispatcher and any script/status page that just
wants a traffic-light answer plus the next cleaner window.

clean_surplus.description: True when the grid looks like clean oversupply now -- renewables dominant, very low carbon, clean margin -- so extra load likely soaks up power that would otherwise be curtailed. The highest-value moment to run flexible jobs. A heuristic, not measured curtailment.
clean_surplus.title: Clean Surplus

cleaner_window_in_hours.description: Hours until a notably cleaner upcoming window, or null if now is fine
cleaner_window_in_hours.title: Cleaner Window In Hours

marginal_basis.description: 'measured' when marginal comes from an operator-configured source (e.g. WattTime MOER), else 'heuristic' (a fuel-mix merit-order estimate).
marginal_basis.title: Marginal Basis

marginal_intensity_gco2_kwh.description: Estimated emissions of an extra kWh of demand now -- the number that actually responds to shifting load. Heuristic from the fuel mix; null when no live fuel mix is available.
marginal_intensity_gco2_kwh.title: Marginal Intensity Gco2 Kwh

marginal_note.description: Plain-English caveat when the marginal picture changes the decision (e.g. clean on average but fossil on the margin), or null when nothing notable.
marginal_note.title: Marginal Note

state.description: green | yellow | red, by absolute intensity thresholds
state.title: State

surplus_window_in_hours.description: Hours until the soonest upcoming clean-surplus window in the forecast (null if none ahead, or if now is already surplus). The best time to shift a flexible job to, even when now is merely 'cleaner'.
surplus_window_in_hours.title: Surplus Window In Hours

CarbonSignal.title: CarbonSignal

CloudUsageRecord.description:
A single cloud resource usage measurement for emissions calculation.

Formula: emissions_kgco2e = energy_kwh * carbon_intensity_gco2_kwh / 1000
Where:   energy_kwh = usage_quantity * energy_coefficient * pue
CloudUsageRecord.title: CloudUsageRecord

Which grids reward carbon-aware scheduling, ranked. High shiftability = a big
intra-day swing, so picking the right hour matters; near zero = it barely helps.

zones.description: Most shiftable first; zones without enough data omitted

SitingRecommendation.description:
Greenest region to permanently host a 24/7 workload, by typical intensity.

Distinct from per-request routing, which optimises the instantaneous value. Region
choice is a permanent, high-leverage decision -- a region can be many times cleaner.

annual_kg_saved_vs_worst.description: Annual kg CO2 saved vs the worst candidate, at the given load
annual_kg_saved_vs_worst.title: Annual Kg Saved Vs Worst

TimeSlot.description: A candidate time window for scheduling.

score.description: Lower is better for carbon, higher for renewable

WeatherConditions.description:
Current weather at a region's coordinates -- the physical drivers behind its
renewable output. Wind turns turbines; sunlight drives solar. A single-point
proxy for a whole grid zone, from Open-Meteo (free, no key).

solar_irradiance_w_m2.description: Shortwave solar irradiance at the surface, in W/m2 (drives solar generation)
solar_irradiance_w_m2.title: Solar Irradiance W M2

wind_speed_kmh.description: Surface wind speed at 10 m, in km/h (drives wind generation)
wind_speed_kmh.title: Wind Speed Kmh

WeatherConditions.title: WeatherConditions

shift_savings_pct.description: How much a daily job would save moving from the worst to the best hour (%)
shift_savings_pct.title: Shift Savings Pct

info.description:
A free, open API for the real-time carbon intensity of cloud regions. No account or API key required; usage is rate-limited so it stays responsive for everyone.

## Carbon Data API
- **6 live grid-operator integrations** (UK, EIA, AEMO, GridStatus, ENTSO-E, Electricity Maps) plus labeled heuristic and weather-based estimates, cascading
- **Grid carbon intensity** for 75+ cloud regions; every response is tagged with its `source`
- **Batch queries** for multiple regions in a single call

## Compliance Reporting
- **GHG-Protocol-structured** Scope 2 (location-based) + Scope 3 Cat 1 reporting
- Drafts aimed at **CSRD / SEC Climate / SB 253** workflows (not an assured report)
- **EU Taxonomy** eligibility flag (simplified screening)

## Green SLA Monitoring (Beta)
- **Define carbon targets** — max gCO2/kWh, min renewable %
- **On-demand + background checks** (in-memory state)
- **Attestation-style summary reports**

## Carbon-Aware Scheduling
- **Find optimal time windows** for batch jobs, CI/CD, ML training
- **Multi-region evaluation** across AWS, GCP, Azure
- **Three strategies** — lowest carbon, highest renewable, balanced
- Returns a recommendation — it doesn't run or defer your workload itself
info.title: Carbon Lens

post.description:
Record one carbon-aware run in the org ledger (the system-of-record).

Hosts running `carbonlens run` POST here so org-statement is live and multi-host.
Persists only when a database is configured; otherwise reports it wasn't stored.

schema.title: Response Ingest Impact Api V1 Accounting Impact Post

get.description:
Methodology-stated org carbon-savings statement from the DB-backed ledger.

Returns an empty (zeroed) statement when no database is configured, so the shape
is stable. Same aggregation/methodology as the `carbonlens org-statement` CLI.

schema.title: Response Get Org Statement Api V1 Accounting Org Statement Get

get.description: Get carbon savings report for all routed workloads.

How this region's intensity compares to its own recent baseline — a
'cleaner/dirtier than usual right now' read from the published history archive.
Returns ``insufficient_history`` until enough has accumulated.

post.description:
Get carbon intensity for multiple regions in a single request.

Body: list of ``{"provider": "aws", "region": "us-east-1"}`` objects.
Returns a map of ``"provider/region"`` to carbon intensity.

schema.title: Response Get Carbon Intensity Batch Api V1 Carbon Batch Post

get.description:
Greenest hour-of-day to schedule a recurring job on a grid zone directly -- for
on-prem / colo workloads. History is read from a representative region on the zone.

parameters.description: Daily job energy (kWh) for an annualized kg-saved estimate.
parameters.name: energy_kwh

schema.description: Daily job energy (kWh) for an annualized kg-saved estimate.
schema.title: Energy Kwh

get.description:
The greenest hour-of-day to run a recurring job here -- pick a cron schedule once.

Ranks UTC hours by mean carbon intensity from the published history archive; if
too little has accumulated, it falls back to the next-48h forecast curve as a
proxy (labelled in ``basis``). Moving a fixed daily job to ``cleanest_hour_utc``
is a one-time change with permanent savings.

parameters.description: Daily job energy (kWh) for an annualized kg-saved estimate.
parameters.name: energy_kwh

schema.description: Daily job energy (kWh) for an annualized kg-saved estimate.
schema.title: Energy Kwh

get.description:
Hour-by-hour carbon-intensity forecast for a cloud region.

EU zones use ENTSO-E's real day-ahead wind/solar/load forecast; elsewhere a
local time-of-day model is used (named in ``method``). The first point is the
current reading.

Past carbon intensity for a cloud region, from the published rolling archive.

Oldest first. The archive is accumulated by the scheduled snapshot builder, so
a region returns points only once it has been observed (empty until then).

parameters.description: How far back to return (1-720 hours).
parameters.name: hours

schema.description: How far back to return (1-720 hours).
schema.title: Hours

get.description:
Which grids reward carbon-aware scheduling, ranked by intra-day swing.

For each covered grid zone, how much a daily job would save by running at its
cleanest hour vs its dirtiest, from the published history. High = shifting pays
off (variable wind/solar grids); near zero = it barely helps (flat grids). Tells
you where to spend the effort. Zones without enough history are omitted.

Run-now/wait decision for a grid zone directly -- for on-prem / colo workloads
that sit on a grid we cover but aren't a cloud region. Use IDs from /carbon/zones.

One-call traffic-light decision: run a flexible job here now, or wait?

Returns a green/yellow/red state plus, when meaningfully cleaner power is
coming, how many hours until that window. The minimal primitive for the
carbon-aware-dispatcher or any script — loosely coupled via a stable contract.

Greenest region to permanently host a 24/7 workload, by *typical* intensity.

Region choice is a permanent, high-leverage decision -- a region can be many
times cleaner forever. Ranks candidates by their history-mean carbon intensity
(falling back to the current reading where history is thin), and -- given a
continuous load -- the annual kg of CO2 each would emit. Distinct from
``/route``, which optimises the *instantaneous* value for a single request.

parameters.description: Continuous load (W) for an annual kg estimate, e.g. one server.
parameters.name: power_watts

schema.description: Continuous load (W) for an annual kg estimate, e.g. one server.
schema.title: Power Watts

parameters.description: History window for the typical mean (days).
parameters.name: days

schema.description: History window for the typical mean (days).
schema.title: Days

get.description:
Current wind speed and solar irradiance at a region's coordinates -- the
weather *driving* its renewable output, so a viewer can see why a grid is clean
or dirty right now. A single-point proxy for the whole zone, from Open-Meteo.

Carbon intensity for a grid zone directly (no cloud region needed) -- for
on-prem / colocation workloads. Use the zone IDs from /carbon/zones.

List all supported electricity grid zones and the cloud regions mapped to each.

Returns an array of objects with ``grid_zone``, ``regions`` (list of
``provider/region`` strings), and ``location`` for the first mapped region.

schema.title: Response List Grid Zones Api V1 Carbon Zones Get

Use this to decide when to run CI/CD pipelines, batch processing,
ML training, or any deferrable workload.

post.description:
Run every SLA check that's due now and persist the results.

This is the durable, scale-to-zero-friendly path: a scheduler (the GitHub
Actions cron in .github/workflows/sla-monitor.yml) POSTs here on a cadence, so
checks happen and persist even when the API would otherwise be idle -- unlike
the in-process monitor loop, which only runs while the service is awake.
Admin-only (X-API-Key must match CARBON_LENS_ADMIN_SECRET).

schema.title: Response Run Due Checks Api V1 Sla Monitor Run Post

post.description:
Start the background SLA monitor for an organization's SLAs.

Note: the in-process monitor only runs while the API is awake. For durable,
scheduled checking on a scale-to-zero host, use POST /monitor/run from a cron.

schema.title: Response Start Monitor Api V1 Sla Monitor Start Post

Readiness probe — returns 200 only when all dependencies are available.
